title: All Achievements & Trophies Guide
seoTitle: All Achievements & Trophies — 007 First Light Guide
excerpt: Every achievement and trophy in 007 First Light, including story unlocks, collection milestones, skill challenges, and hidden requirements. Learn the fastest path to Platinum and 100% completion.
metaDescription: Complete 007 First Light achievements and trophies guide covering all 38 unlocks, Platinum difficulty rating, story achievements, collectible milestones, skill challenges, hidden trophies, and the fastest completion route.

sections.heading: Achievement Overview: What You Are Chasing
body: 007 First Light ships with 38 achievements on Steam (39 trophies on PlayStation, including the Platinum). The full list breaks down into four categories: Story & Progression (14), Collection & Discovery (8), Skill & Technique (10), and Hidden & Secret (6). The game’s achievement design follows IO Interactive’s philosophy of rewarding mastery rather than grinding, which means most achievements are obtainable through smart routing rather than repetitive farming.

Platinum difficulty is rated 5/10 by the community. The hardest achievements involve completing specific mission sections under S-Rank conditions with restricted loadouts, and collecting all Golden Gun parts across three missions. The entire achievement set can be completed in approximately 25-30 hours of focused play, or 35-40 hours for a first-time player learning the game’s systems.

This guide organises achievements by category and provides the fastest unlock route for each one. We recommend focusing on story achievements during your first playthrough, then returning for skill challenges and collectibles once you have unlocked all gadgets and mission select.
sections.quote: 007 First Light respects your time. Most achievements reward understanding, not repetition.

sections.heading: Story & Progression Achievements
body: These 14 achievements unlock automatically as you complete missions and reach progression milestones. None are missable — every story achievement can be earned on any difficulty setting. Complete the campaign once and you will have all 14.
• Rookie Agent — Complete Mission 1. 10G / Bronze. Unlocks after the opening cutscene. Cannot be missed.
• Deep Diver — Complete Mission 2: Deep Water. 10G / Bronze.
• Silo Survivor — Complete Mission 3: Silo Break. 15G / Bronze.
• Vault Cracker — Complete Mission 4: The Vault. 15G / Bronze.
• Data Trader — Complete Mission 5: The Exchange. 20G / Silver.
• Frost Walker — Complete Mission 6: Cold Storage. 20G / Silver.
• Signal Hunter — Complete Mission 7. 25G / Silver.
• Subzero Protocol — Complete Mission 8. 25G / Silver.
• License Renewed — Complete Mission 9 and the campaign. 50G / Gold.
• Gadget Collector — Unlock all 8 core gadgets. 15G / Bronze. Earned naturally through mission progression.
• Workshop Upgrade — Fully upgrade one gadget to tier 3. 10G / Bronze. Spend upgrade points on a single gadget path.
• Arsenal Ready — Fully upgrade all gadgets. 40G / Gold. Requires completing all intel collection and spending all upgrade points.
• First TacSim Clear — Complete one TacSim mission with any rank. 10G / Bronze.
• Simulation Veteran — Complete all available TacSim missions at launch. 30G / Silver. Requires clearing every simulation on the TacSim map select screen.
tips: The Arsenal Ready achievement requires the most playtime. Prioritise intel-rich routes during missions to maximise gadget upgrade resources.
Simulation Veteran is easier on Normal difficulty. Ignore S-Rank and focus on completion.

sections.heading: Collection & Discovery Achievements
body: These 8 achievements reward thorough exploration. Most can be earned during free play after completing the campaign. The Golden Gun achievements are the most time-sensitive because the parts are in specific mission locations.

• Intel Hoarder — Collect 25 intel caches across the campaign. 15G / Bronze. Intel caches are marked on the mission select screen after discovery. Use free play to revisit missions and sweep each zone.

• Audio Archivist — Collect all audio logs. 15G / Bronze. There are 18 audio logs across 9 missions — two per mission. The second log in each mission is usually in the extraction zone and easy to miss.
• Golden Gun Initiate — Collect Golden Gun Part 1 (Mission 4: The Vault). 10G / Bronze. See our [Mission 4 guide](/guides/mission-4-the-vault) for the exact location.
• Golden Gun Assembler — Collect Golden Gun Part 2 (Mission 5: The Exchange). 10G / Bronze.
• Golden Gun Craftsman — Collect Golden Gun Part 3 (Mission 6: Cold Storage). 10G / Bronze.
• Licence to Kill — Assemble the full Golden Gun. 30G / Silver. Unlocks after collecting all three parts and completing the assembly in the Q Branch menu.
• Sightseer — Visit all named locations on the world map. 10G / Bronze. Every mission and TacSim map counts. Complete all campaign missions and TacSims to unlock.
• Weapon Blueprint Collector — Collect all prototype weapon schematics. 25G / Silver. Schematics are found in Missions 3, 6, and 8. Each schematic unlocks a bonus gadget recipe.

callouts.title: Golden Gun priority
callouts.content: Make collecting all three Golden Gun parts your top side objective. The weapon is powerful enough to change how you approach Missions 7-9. Collect Parts 1-3 during your first clear of Missions 4-6 and assemble immediately.

sections.heading: Skill & Technique Achievements
body: These 10 achievements test your mechanical skill and route knowledge. Most require planning and repetition rather than raw reflexes. The hardest are the S-Rank achievements for Missions 5 and 9.

• Ghost — Complete any mission without raising a single alert. 20G / Silver. An alert includes suspicion, investigation, and combat states. Mission 1 is the easiest target because of its simple patrol layout.

• Invisible Hand — Complete any mission without any takedowns. 20G / Silver. Mission 4 (The Vault) is the best choice because the S-Rank route already minimises takedowns. Time all patrols and use gadgets for distraction.
• Silent Professional — Complete any mission with S-Rank. 20G / Silver. Mission 1 and Mission 2 have the most forgiving S-Rank requirements. Use our walkthroughs for the exact route.

• Exchange Master — Complete Mission 5 (The Exchange) with S-Rank. 25G / Silver. The Exchange has tight timing requirements. See our [S-Rank walkthrough](/guides/mission-5-exchange) for the optimised route.

• Cold Blooded — Complete Mission 6 (Cold Storage) without triggering the temperature alarm. 20G / Silver. Requires perfect acclimation management and coolant valve use. Do not skip any acclimation room cooldown.

• Perfect Hack — Complete a mission with zero failed hack attempts. 10G / Bronze. Hacks are one-attempt-only. If you fail a hack, reload the checkpoint before the failed attempt. Mission 3 has the fewest hack interactions.

• Toolbelt — Use every gadget type in a single mission. 15G / Bronze. Equip all three gadget slots with different types. Use each one at least once during the mission. Mission 5 has enough gadget opportunities.
• Speed Demon — Complete any mission under 8 minutes. 20G / Silver. Mission 1 is the shortest mission and the best candidate. Skip all collectibles and take the most direct route.
• No Witnesses — Complete a mission where every guard has been pacified or evaded. 15G / Bronze. This counts the entire mission population. Mission 3 has the smallest total guard count at 12.

• Leaderboard Contender — Reach top 10% on any TacSim leaderboard. 30G / Silver. Focus on a single simulation and optimise through repetition. See our [TacSim Advanced Guide](/guides/tacsim-advanced-leaderboard-guide) for scoring strategies.
tips: Ghost, Invisible Hand, and Silent Professional can all be earned in a single run of Mission 1. It takes about 10 minutes with our walkthrough route.
Speed Demon pairs well with Ghost. Use Mission 1 and skip all optional objectives.

callouts.title: Skill achievement stacking
callouts.content: You can stack Ghost + Invisible Hand + Silent Professional + Speed Demon in a single Mission 1 run. That is 70G/4 trophies in under 8 minutes. Plan the route carefully using our walkthrough and practice the timing twice before attempting the combined run.

sections.heading: Hidden & Secret Achievements
body: Six achievements in 007 First Light are hidden from the in-game list. The game reveals the achievement title only after you meet the unlock condition. This section names every hidden achievement and the exact trigger condition.

• The Moneypenny Protocol (Hidden) — 15G / Bronze. Complete a mission where you never use a gadget. Equip a loadout with three gadget slots but use none of them. Mission 2 is the easiest because environmental gadgets are plentiful.

• Q’s Favourite (Hidden) — 15G / Bronze. Complete a mission where you use only gadgets and never take a direct action (no takedowns, no hacks, no manual interactions). Mission 5 works well if you use the Advanced Hacking Device for camera disables and distractions for guard manipulation.

• The Old Ways (Hidden) — 20G / Silver. Complete any mission without using Q Lens or Instinct. This requires memorised patrol routes. Mission 1 is the best choice because the layout is simpler. Practice the route three times with HUD, then attempt blind.

• Across the Rooftops (Hidden) — 15G / Bronze. Complete the outdoor traversal section of Mission 8 without touching the ground. Stay on rooftops, scaffolding, and catwalks for the entire outdoor zone. Use the grapple points marked on the environment.

• Double-0 Status (Hidden) — 40G / Gold. Earn S-Rank on all 9 campaign missions. This is the hardest achievement in the game and a prerequisite for Platinum. Use our mission-by-mission walkthroughs to optimise each route. Expect 15-20 hours of focused practice.

• Classic MI6 (Hidden) — 30G / Silver. Complete the campaign on the highest difficulty setting. Enemies have faster detection, reduced suspicion recovery time, and tighter patrol windows. The gadget upgrades and route knowledge from lower difficulties transfer directly. Complete your first run on Normal, then tackle Classic.

callouts.title: Hidden achievement strategy
callouts.content: Hidden achievements are designed to be discovered organically, but most can be intentionally farmed in 30 minutes. Tackle them after completing your story run. Double-0 Status should be your final achievement target before the Platinum.

sections.heading: Fastest Completion Route (35-Hour Plan)
body: The most efficient path to 100% completion follows a four-phase plan. Phase 1 (10 hours): Complete the campaign on Normal difficulty. Do not chase collectibles or S-Rank. Learn the maps and unlock all gadgets. The story achievements alone are worth 230G.

Phase 2 (8 hours): Replay missions for collectibles. Use mission select and free play to collect all intel caches, audio logs, Golden Gun parts, and weapon schematics. Start with Missions 4-6 for the Golden Gun. The assembled weapon will make Phases 3 and 4 significantly easier.

Phase 3 (10 hours): Earn skill and hidden achievements. Stack compatible achievements in single runs — Ghost + Invisible Hand + Silent Professional in Mission 1, Speed Demon in Mission 2, Perfect Hack in Mission 3. Then tackle the hidden achievements one by one.

Phase 4 (7 hours): Double-0 Status and Classic MI6. Replay each mission for S-Rank using our walkthroughs. Start with your strongest mission and build momentum. For Classic MI6, play conservatively — the faster detection punishes aggressive routes. Use distraction gadgets liberally.
The Platinum unlocks after Double-0 Status and Classic MI6. Total time: approximately 35 hours using this phased approach. If you enjoy TacSim, add 5 hours for the Leaderboard Contender achievement.
sections.quote: The Platinum is earned through smart routing, not grinding. Every achievement in First Light has a clean path.

faq.question: How many hidden achievements does 007 First Light have?
faq.answer: Six hidden achievements. Their titles and conditions are revealed only after unlocking. We have listed all six with their exact trigger conditions above.

faq.question: Can achievements be earned in free play mode?
faq.answer: Yes. Mission select and free play both count for all achievements. The only exception is Classic MI6, which requires a full campaign playthrough on the highest difficulty.

faq.question: What is the hardest achievement in the game?
faq.answer: Double-0 Status (S-Rank on all 9 missions) is the hardest, requiring optimised routing and consistent execution across every mission. Classic MI6 is difficult but more forgiving because it only requires completion, not perfection.

faq.question: Is the Platinum achievement considered hard?
faq.answer: Community rating is 5/10. The difficulty comes from the Double-0 Status requirement rather than any single impossible challenge. With proper routing, most players can achieve it within 35 hours.
